releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0 Patch
releases.body:
# Patch & Patch Generation (GameHub v5.1.0)

The files needed to contribute to the project and patch GameHub 5.1.0 yourself are now released. 

- Completely reworked the initial patching approach.
- *Revanced* patch is a work in progress, feel free to contribute to this if you are comfortable with revanced. 
- Easy update patches after modifications 
- Work on macOS/Linux. (maybe windows?)

Read the [README](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/blob/master/README.md) file for a full explanation.

### FAQ

- Does this work with GameHub version *not 5.1.0*
- **no.**


--- 


## How It Works

The patcher uses a multi-step process:

1. **Decompile** - Uses apktool to decompile the original APK to smali bytecode
2. **Delete** - Removes telemetry SDKs, unused assets, and tracking libraries
3. **Patch** - Applies unified diff patches to modify smali code
4. **Add** - Copies new files (resources, additional smali)
5. **Rebuild** - Reassembles the APK using apktool
6. **Sign** - Signs with a debug keystore for installation

## Patch Contents

| Category | Count | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Deletions | 3,385 | Telemetry SDKs, tracking code, unused assets |
| Additions | 2,856 | New resources, modified assets, new features |
| Modifications | 223 | Smali code patches for behavior changes |

### Removed Components

- **Native libraries**: libumeng-spy.so, libcrashsdk.so, libalicomphonenumberauthsdk_core.so, etc.
- **SDKs**: Umeng Analytics, JPush, Firebase Analytics, Tencent login
- **Assets**: Splash video, auth videos, emoji font (saves ~30MB)
- **Permissions**: Location, contacts, phone state, ad tracking

### Added Features

- Custom splash/intro video from community CDN
- Local game ID copy functionality
- Offline mode improvements
- News page integration with community API

## For Developers

### Regenerating Patches

If you've modified the Lite APK and want to update the patches:

```bash
./generate-patches.sh [path/to/original.apk] [path/to/lite.apk]
```

This will:
1. Decompile both APKs
2. Generate diff patches for modified files
3. Copy new files to patches directory
4. Create deletion and addition lists

### Patch Directory Structure

```
patches/
├── files_to_delete.txt        # List of files to remove
├── files_to_add.txt             # List of files to add
├── files_to_patch.txt          # List of files to modify
├── diffs/                                # Unified diff patches
│   ├── AndroidManifest.xml.patch
│   ├── smali/...
│   └── res/...
├── new_files/                        # New files to add
│   ├── res/...
│   └── smali_classes10/...     # Robot language files to add
└── stats.txt                           # Patch statistics (ignored and generated on patch-generation)
```

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0
releases.body:
A restoration build of GameHub Lite V4 before everything fell apart. I will publish the code for the workers and a patch very soon, for now enjoy a working GameHub Lite app again.

We'll focus on restoring the previous functionality first, before fixing and adding functionality. So please only report issues that were not present in GameHub Lite V4 or in the official GameHub app.

---

### Explanation for the different versions

You’ll often see an emulator distribute different versions with a name suffixed like **PUBG**, **Antutu**, **Optimized**, **Genshin**, **Ludashi** and other names. Here follows an explanation for what is what, is still has slight differences between manufacturers and even different packagenames but it should give you a rough understanding.

—-

*The different versions are identical. What we do is a very old android trick to gain extra performance on some devices.*

**Antutu**

Some manufacturers “cheat” by setting the governor to performance when they detect the Antutu package name. 

*Nerd explanation:*

The CPU governor essentially controls the CPU's frequency scaling. allowing it to operate at different clock speeds and voltages based on the system load. So making the CPU go fast for sustained usage, what is actually made for peak usage. This comes with a risk of overheating, but I don’t believe in this. Android does a well enough job of thermal management and makes it extremely hard for software to exceed what the hardware is capable of and damaging itself.


That said, it’s still extra heat. More heat == more bad. I just think it’s negligible, especially if your device has a fan.


**PUBG**

On a high level it’s the same as Antutu, but some slight differences that only benefit games. Think of network prioritization and touch input latency improvements. The manufacturers goal when they detect Antutu is **ALL THE POWER**. Benchmarks are relatively short and it makes them look better on comparison websites.


The goal for PUBG is more like **MORE POWER**, since the intention is often to have a game running for longer it has less aggressive changes.


**TLDR and summary:**

**Antutu** spoofing:

    •    Maximum CPU/GPU frequencies unlocked  
    •    Aggressive performance governors  
    •    Short-duration performance boost (benchmark workload)  
    •    Thermal limits are less strict   
    •    All cores available  


**PUBG** and other games spoofing: 

    •    Sustained gaming performance profiles  
    •    GPU driver optimizations (Adreno/Mali game-specific paths)  
    •    Frame pacing and scheduling improvements  
    •    Reduced touch latency   
    •    Network QoS prioritization  
    •    Different thermal management (sustained vs burst)  
    •    Qualcomm “Game Performance Mode”      
    •    Sometimes enables features like frame-gen


**Ludashi** spoofing:

    •    Similar to Antutu but slightly less aggressive    
    •    Longer sustained performance boost (multi-minute tests)   
    •    Memory frequency optimization

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.0.0
releases.body:
This is a temporary release that restores most of the functionality previously available in GameHub Lite v4.

GameHub Lite v4 stopped working after the former developer took down the Cloudflare Workers it relied on, citing privacy concerns. 

We are currently developing a community-funded solution to provide a more stable and permanent setup.

In the meantime, this version includes most of the core features but may display some visual inconsistencies and lacks certain extras that were present in v4, such as the news and free games sections.

Special thanks to **The412Banner** and **Trixarian**!

[Join the EmuReady Discord for support and more information](https://discord.gg/CYhCzApXav)

releases.name: v0.8.1
releases.body:
## What's Changed

- Added Steam achievement support!
- Correctly install prerequisites like VCRedist, PhysX, XNA, etc by @unbelievableflavour
- Add quick menu performance HUD overlay by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/777
- Fix bug for Steam downloads where they get stuck on "Resume" in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/767
- Fix local saves being overwritten with cloud saves if connection lost during gameplay by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/791
- Fixed infinite booting screens, black screens on boot and improved ability to download contents from our endpoints
- Fixes Edit Container crashes on older Android versions by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/753
- Fix external game launches while app is open & avoid pop-ups on external launch by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/759
- Added container config export/import for all platforms by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/649
- Added per-game auto-pause/resume settings by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/746
- Fixed VirGL in Glibc containers - Will enable Mali users to play 2D games in Glibc by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/763
- Fixed touchpad input by @AndreVto in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/725
- Added language-support for gen 1 GOG downloader by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/743
- Added language support for Epic (also shrinks game sizes) by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/754
- Replace Toast with Compose Snackbar system by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/697
- Show games on internal and external storage at the same time by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/781
- Allow searching for game titles with diacritics removed by @danbruno in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/712
- add fexcore 2603 by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/723
- remove trim for passwords to fix trailing spaces in some user passwords by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/736
- Fix: Closing FileInputStream and BufferedReader on listRunningWineProcesses by @giovannygb in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/756
- refactor: deduplicate network connectivity checks by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/648
- Fix app crash in race condition in DownloadInfo ETA by snapshotting speed samples by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/755
- fix: resume immediately when opening keyboard in manual suspend mode by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/779
- Translation into Russian by @Denis11212 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/695

Bugfixes from v0.8.1-prerelease:
* fix: skip achievements generation when Steam not logged in by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/835
* fix: allow intent launches for non-Steam and offline-mode games by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/832
* Added app screen gradient so back button stays visible by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/843
* Fix/cpu affinity empty selection crash by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/764
* fix: wait for exit sync before returning to launcher by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/792
* fix(): Epic name mismatch between game and container ID + added logging for container deletion to catch orphans by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/760
* Fix crash on empty depot list (#729) by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/772
* fix: update action shows Resume instead of download progress by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/806
* Updated achievement uploading code to not skip achievements.json when… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/847
* Cleaned up UI on Portrait screens (on game page) and on small landsca… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/862
* Consolidated networking code to always attempt Google DNS, should pre… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/861
* Handle XServerView lifecycle across app sleep by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/853
* fix: SD card games unrecoverable when external storage toggle off by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/809

## New Contributors
* @Denis11212 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/695
* @danbruno made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/712
* @Catpotatos made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/753
* @giovannygb made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/756

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/compare/v0.8.0...v0.8.1

releases.name: v0.8.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* [HOTFIX] Added back Reverse Landscape and setting allowed orientations
* Complete UI/UX overhaul by @ObfuscatedVoid , @phobos665, @xXJSONDeruloXx and me (in chronological order)
* Added Amazon games support by @xXJSONDeruloXx 
* Added dynamic sticks for touchscreen controller (left and right sticks will show up on touch) + a new touchscreen preset for strategy games by @Nightwalker743 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/599
* Improve Cloud Saves for Epic Games by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/589
* Fixed external mouse by @jeremybernstein and @AndreVto 
* Fix rumble on devices such as Ayn Odin 2 by @kiequoo in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/538
* Various compatibility and bugfixes for GOG by @unbelievableflavour
* Correctly download audio after changing language for Steam games by @joshuatam in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/507
* Correctly download audio after changing language for GOG games by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/592
* Several bugfixes, including correctly applying FEX preset, crashes with large logs and custom exes, fixing fetching of SteamGridDB images, fixing affinity not being applied, downloading missing proton on first install and more by @jeremybernstein 
* Fixed L2 and R2 being registered as buttons instead of axes
* Pause games on bringing up in-game menu by @xXJSONDeruloXx 
* Added better support for pocket taco/8bitdo flippad type portrait controllers in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/541
* Add new manifest attribute to ensure app is classified as a game - will help with Samsung Game Launcher by @Unnvaldr in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/709
* Added steam offline mode for games like N++
* Update pulseaudio component for fixing loss of audio when pausing by @joshuatam in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/534
* Fixed second-screen keyboard staying visible for AYN Thor by @xXJSONDeruloXx 
* fix(l10n): Refine Spanish translations and fix a small UI bug by @zeedif in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/511
* Add Korean (ko) locale support by @TeamConceptKR in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/512
* Feedback for non-steam games now supported by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/577
* add toggle for auto-apply known config by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/586
* use header image instead of capsule image if missing for steam games by @silentrald in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/555
* Hotfix for Polish Language to be included in build.gradle.kts by @Petexy in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/529

## New Contributors
* @TeamConceptKR made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/512
* @xXJSONDeruloXx made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/531
* @kiequoo made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/538
* @silentrald made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/555
* @Nightwalker743 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/599
* @AndreVto made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/664
* @Unnvaldr made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/709
* @ObfuscatedVoid made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/700

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/compare/v0.7.2...v0.8.0

releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Always apply known config on first launch of steam. No more tweaking! in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/499
* Improved GOG & Epic Games auth flow. No more need for code pasting! by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/480
* Improves download and decompression performance by @joshuatam in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/425
* Adds new FEXCore presets by @joshuatam in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/492
* Epic download and DRM improvements by @phobos665
* add Spanish localization support by @zeedif in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/434
* Fix image fetch support by @Lobanokivan11 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/479
* Working Game Shortcuts for all game sources by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/423
* Feat: Adds game-specific save location symlink support by @joshuatam in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/441
* Polish Translations for GameNative by @Petexy in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/491
* Fix/cloud save for GameInstall folder by @silentrald in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/508

## What's Changed
* Epic library support by @phobos665

releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Added ability to add custom games by @maaggel
* Show compatibility of games within GameNative, allow applying of known configs for games
* Cleaned up and fixed download issues, estimated time is far more accurate and download UI is better
* Added new wrapper driver, significantly improve compatibility for non-Adreno devices, reduce glitches, increase performance in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/296
* Added Proton 10 as downloadable content, added ability to include custom wine and proton wcps by @phobos665 
* Brought in some new Turnip Drivers and Box64 by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/294
* Added pull down to refresh gamelist by @maaggel in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/308
* Added beta support for Denuvo
* Added Brazilian Portuguese, Danish and Simplified Chinese translations by @rodrigosiviero, @maaggel, @darkcl, @phobos665 
* Fixed crashes when using external storage external storage by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/317
* Correctly pause and resume games when app paused or screen turned off by @Phr3d13  in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/322

releases.name: v0.5.2
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Reduced heat while downloading, made downloads faster, fixed games that would not download previously by integrating JavaSteam 1.8.0. Thanks @LossyDragon!
* Overhauled DRM to make more games work (eg Halo MCC)
* Reduced crashes, improved app responsiveness generally
* Added automatic updates in-app, no more needing to go to GitHub
* Use avg frame rate and session length for compatibility list
* Fixed games that have both 32-bit and 64-bit depots by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/206
* Add com.oculus.vrshellsupports_free_resizing to allow window resizing on Oculus Quest headsets by @goopsie in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/221
* Added option to go into offline mode manually in app
* Allow downloads over ethernet
* Small fix for launching via Steam client
* Initial support for multiple languages by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249
* dxvk-2.7.1 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/261
* FEXCore-2511 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/253
* game title list borders by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/247
* Added functionality to hide topbar when not in game by @Maaggel in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/242

## New Contributors
* @goopsie made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/221
* @jeremybernstein made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/206
* @phobos665 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249

* improved error handling while downloading

releases.name: v0.5.2-prerelease2
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Added option to go into offline mode manually
* Store session length and avg frame rate when submitting feedback
* Allow downloads over ethernet
* Fix for launch with steam client
* Reduced heat while downloading, made downloads faster, fixed games that would not download previously by integrating JavaSteam 1.8.0. Thanks @LossyDragon!
* Migrate all UI Strings to String Templates to enable Localisation Work by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249
* Overhauled DRM to make more games work (eg Halo MCC)
* dxvk-2.7.1 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/261
* FEXCore-2511 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/253
* Show game name in grid/hero view by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/247
* Added functionality to hide topbar when not in game by @Maaggel in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/242

## New Contributors
* @phobos665 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/compare/v0.5.2-prerelease1...v0.5.2-prerelease2

releases.name: v0.4.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Migrated containers to handle non-Steam stores by @unbelievableflavour 
* Fixed PulseAudio
* Fixed downloading of languages
* Fixed downloading of DLC
* Fixed bug where games would stop working after an update
* Fixed L2 & R2 not working on some devices
* Added option use touchscreen
* Added option to completely disable mouse input
* Fix crashes caused by corrupted data or incomplete downloads
* Major compatibility fixes for blackscreens
* Added controller navigation + prevent controllers focusing the search bar by default by @RadicalDog in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/129
* Display supporters from Kofi within app
* Fixed controller B button acting as back button on some devices
* Added ability to "hot swap" controllers (can now add controllers once the game is running)
* Added ability to put app in background or lock screen and come back to it
* New drivers, dx wrappers


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/compare/gamenative-v0.3.4...gamenative-v0.4.0

releases.name: Sumi Emulator - Vulkan API Upgrade [1.1 -> 1.3/1.4], SPIR-V 1.6, Memory Management, Fixed Memory Leaks
releases.body:
**Sumi Emulator** - Custom Vulkan API Upgrade [1.1 -> 1.3/1.4] and SPIR-V 1.6 Base Integration + Memory Management
---
### Release Notes: 

**Sumi 0.9.4** -  Custom Vulkan API Upgrade [1.1 -> 1.3/1.4] and SPIR-V 1.6 Base Integration + Memory Management - General;
_Major upgrade in the most important external modules of emulator, like Vulkan API or SPIR-V - adapted by us, FFMPEG, sirit, SDL, Change Scheduler Priority to make it less thermal throttling (wait for v0.9.5 - full thermal throttling fix). Fixed the softlock in many games - especially Mario Kart._

**Memory Leaks:**
_Fixed Memory Leaks in big demanding games like ToTK or BotW._

**Refactored X64:**
_Refactored and fixed whole Windows X64 build to fix all known bugs._

**Intelligent Frame Limiting:**
_Sumi v0.9.4 has implemented "intelligent" frame limiting - when it detect that the game should or can only work in 30FPS or 60FPS, it limit it to this framerate. This technology will be way more enhanced after frame pacing/skipping fixes._

---
### Nearest Future:


**Thermal Throttling:**
I know, some of you have a problem with temperature and I'm really mad about that. Good news is, that I'm currently working on v0.9.5, where on my milestones are temperature management, which is currently is amazing in comparison to current version. And after that I will be consistently working on frame skipping and better thread management, which with limiting framerate nay lead to stable temperature and also better performance. Before release 1.0.0 there will be next-gen "upscaling" with AMD FidelityFX 3 / AMD FidelityFX CAS. We are experimenting with it now!

---

Sumi Team :)

---

### Suggested Drivers:

Best for Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 - _and similar ones_:
https://github.com/K11MCH1/AdrenoToolsDrivers/releases
Latest: Turnip_v25.X.X_RX_Auto
**DEFINITELY *AUTO* OR *FIXED* NOT *GMEM* !**

### Snapdragon 8 Elite / Adreno 830:

There are still no proper drivers for 8 Elite, but we trying all our best, to provide the Switch emulation for every user. So, we started our internal project to provide emulation drivers for 8 Elite. Stay tuned :)

---

- Version v0.9.4 x64 - FIX2 has fixed all Input System problems! 

- Due to false accusations that we have stolen the Vulkan API implementation from Eden, I have just added our Vulkan API implementation source code here, before I will find good place on some Git hosting. We use Citron-based 1.1/1.2, not Yuzu like Eden, and it is generally as different as it can. It will be shared 

---

_Zelda BotW with graphical enhancement mod - 47.2FPS:_
![1000014780](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/749d18b8-7926-4898-b622-058f21e126d1)

releases.name: Sumi Emulator - Thermal Throttling and CPU Limiting Update + License Verification Fix
releases.body:
**Sumi Emulator** - Thermal Throttling and CPU Limiting Update + License Verification Fix
---
### Release Notes: 

**Sumi 0.9.3** -  Thermal Throttling and CPU Limiting Update + License Verification Fix
_Small, pdate, that changes the threads affinity and priority to do not overheat devices if it is possible. It may slow emulation a little bit, but provide way better thermal state. Fixed APK license verification problem, especially on older Android devices._

---

Removed some experimental improvements from Sumi 0.9.2. Will be bringed back in Sumi 0.9.5.
Credits to better frame pacing method: Eden Emulator

---

Sumi Team :)

releases.name: Sumi v.0.9.2 -  CPU Speedup Update - Android + Windows
releases.body:
**Sumi Emulator** - CPU Speedup Update - Android + Windows - 0.9.2
---
### Release Notes: 

**Sumi 0.9.2** - **EXPERIMENTAL** - CPU Emulation Enhancement
_Small, experimental update, that provides, way better CPU<->GPU communication and CPU emulation.
For example, on Android BoTW, instead of - previous - stable 30 FPS+ on Snapdragon Gen 3 this game has around 40-50 FPS._

It is only temporary enhancement, due to the fact that we needs to solve previouse issues and make some project cleanup, before this caliber upgrade. 
It may come back in v0.9.5. 

#### **Also, this is an EXPERIMENTAL upgrade, to check if this way is really proper.**
Currentely on hidden branch.

---

This is **UNSTABLE** and experimental version of emulator. It may cause issues on some devices. If you want the stable release, download Sumi v0.9.1. If it works well - good for you! :)

---

Versions x64 FIX1 and/or FIX2 should have fixed input system, gamepad support and Xinput.

---

P.S.
We are considering bringing back the OpenGL rendering.
Public Linux and MacOS in progress! Will be available soon!

releases.name: Sumi - DLC Installation Manager
releases.body:
DLC Installation Solution 
---

We have found issue with installing DLC on many titles. If you want to install DLC, the temporary solution may be:
1. Installing last Sumi version (v 0.3.0) where problem with DLC does not occur.
2. Installing the DLC you want. 
3. Installing the latest version again and be happy with your installed DLCs!

---

It's only temporary solution. We are working on fixing the issue, but if someone needs the DLCs at the moment, there is solution!

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v24
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V24  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8. Buh buh buh

I didn't test this this much. Contact me on FEX (#android-wastelands) or EmuCore discord if there are any issues.

>[!WARNING]
> Please don't report issues to Mesa, PLEASE
          
Supported GPUs:
* Adreno 8XX Family: 840, 830, 829, 825 (please test this one, it might be broken), 810
* Adreno 7XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip + 710/720 from Termux patches
* Adreno 6XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Adreno 810 is additionally very slow, I'm not sure if you even want to use Turnip there

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* GMEM rendering produces glitchy picture on A830 (write page faults), disable it with `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. It's pretty much useless for PC emulation anyway.

What's new:
* Upstream rebase (lots of changes from there)
* All A8XX GPU variants do not have "Turnip" prefix anymore (upstream)
* Better configs for A829 and A810 (based on upstream non-merged MRs, still better than my untested blindly added config entries)

flushall patch is included. KGSL timeline MR is not merged here due to Draft status.

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git_3
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git_3  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A830/A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes:
- Import upstream as usual
- Decrease reg_size on 830 to 96 - should fix Cyberpunk 2077 crashes. See the commit info.
- Integrate [unmerged](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/merge_requests/39751) KGSL timeline sync MR - works good enough now. This is a proper replacement for old cringy vibecoded timeline sync emulation code I removed few releases ago. 
- Remove gmem cache offset completely as it breaks on lower-end A8XX GPUs.
- due to that, change cache sizes for A825/A810. GMEM should work on A825 now.
- Integrate zdobersek's patch fixing (workaround'ing) FLUSHALL. There's one build now as flushall is disabled by default and fixed.

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes compared to v23:
* Upstream Mesa
* Now doing new builds by applying my patches up on upstream Mesa-git branch instead of cloning my repo (I have updated it as well)
* Removed vibecoded timeline sync emulation shit and other unneeded hacks (read the commit message before blaming me for using AI AI AI)
* Started doing flushall & noflushall builds - flushall is still enabled for A8XX for various reasons
* Various changes in freedreno/tu from Mesa (still no proper A830 config)
* Disabled thin LTO and LTO in general (apparently causes weird bugs in RADV, might also apply for Turnip)
* Maybe something else...

Didn't test this release on my device, but should work well.  

I've also seen interesting Turnip builds with some "cool" fixes, but neither me interested in looking into patches (in sense of asking for sources or searching the repo) nor the author wants to share `git format-patch` of their fork (FWIW I shared all my patches in specific Telegram group), so I care less.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v23
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V23  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

>[!WARNING]
> This release is still Beta, especially for devices other than Adreno 840. PLEASE, don't report any issues to Mesa
> Original merge request is Draft again, so it's not ready
          
Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 810 (tested, working, less stable than all other GPUs) - sysmem only
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Adreno 810 is additionally very slow, I'm not sure if you even want to use Turnip there

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`

Changes:
* Import upstream (another rebase, but nothing new there)
* Disable lrz again. It was causing problems on all A8XX except A840, maybe it's early to enable it there. As Rob said, other subgenerations (A8XX has two: Gen1 & Gen2) may have different layout so A830 specific support is required before enabling it back. This has nothing to do with flushall and shouldn't cause huge performance drops anyway.
* Try removing "raytracing disabled" notice in Adreno 810 device string. This is because RT/BVH instructions were fused-off on an A810, but these are enabled in the GPU definitions since 7xx_gen3.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v20
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V20  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

>[!WARNING]
> This release is still Beta, especially for devices other than Adreno 840. PLEASE, don't report any issues to Mesa
> Original merge request is Draft again, so it's not ready

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 810 (tested, working at least in TestD3D) - might be sysmem-only, didn't test gmem
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem

Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:  
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found). It works fine with drm-shim, so maybe same for real devices?

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Additionally A810 cache size is awfully small, so don't expect magic performance on Turnip.

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Import upstream changes
* Add proper initial support for Adreno 810. It's now rendering a cube!  
* Add support for A710/A720. These were full copies of A730 with only different ids in termux mesa patches.

The issue with A810 is its very small gmem cache (576KB). This caused an integer underflow, because nobody thought of A810 and its so-small cache, breaking rendering completely as invalid value was written onto `RB_CCU_CACHE_CNTL` register. I have discovered this with a debug build of Mesa, it was hitting an assert even with drm-shim due to this underflow.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX vgralloc-exp
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan Vgralloc-exp (V14)

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 810 (had blackscreen as I assumed it's duo-slice, now the rendering should work, but I'm not sure)
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
* ~~gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point~~

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* ~~Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1` Not required on GameHub~~
* ~~Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825~~
Now disabled automatically
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Added a workaround to fix u_gralloc skipping setting drm format modifier type - all weird pixel artifacts without WRAPPER_BLIT=1 should be gone now!

More info:
Fallback gralloc implementation in Mesa has this check to set proper drm format modifier for ubwc/nonubwc swapchain images (ubwc is a Qualcomm compression):
```c
   if (hnd->handle->numInts >= 2 && hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds] == gmsm) {
      /* This UBWC flag was introduced in a5xx. */
      bool ubwc = hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds + 1] & 0x08000000;
      out->modifier = ubwc ? DRM_FORMAT_MOD_QCOM_COMPRESSED : DRM_FORMAT_MOD_LINEAR;
   }
```
Looks like a8xx changed something here thus this condition fails and drm format isn't being set at all. This was the reason of garbled output in most native Vulkan apps.  
Removing the check is enough to overcome this issue.

* Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1`
* **Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without**: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz` or `TU_DEBUG=sysmem,nolrz` if A830.

What's new in V11:
* Reverted the tessellation draw skip hack (TS fixed in upstream)
* Switched to GH CI (won't affect builds, simply a convenience change)

Improved HD mode quality and performances
* Fix cores not downloaded in the Github build

* Migrate mobile UI to Jetpack Compose
* Improved HD upscaling algorithm
* Updated cores
* Fix UI issues in Home
* Minor fixes

* Introduced a new HD mode
* Updated cores
* Updated databases

* Improve UX when changing game-pad bindings
* Greatly improves directory scanning performances
* Minor UX improvements to the TV version
* Improved overall responsiveness of the application (no changes in games)
* Fixes build issues caused by translations

* Update cores
* Update games database
* Better handling of Sega CD and NDS bioses
* Expose more settings in DS and 3DS
* Minor fixes and improvements

* Add HD filter to efficiently upscale games
* Updated translations

* Improve visibility and style of touch controls
* Improve touch controls positions on device with notch
* Expose "Accurate Shader Multiplication" setting on 3DS
* Fix some gamepads no longer detected
* Fixed haptics on virtual key release on some devices

* Improve spacing of touch layouts
* Fix mixed Stick and DPAD events
* Fix some flickering on some DS and N64 games
* Improve detection of gamepads
* Fixed a couple of crashes

* Introduce Material You design with dynamic colors on Android 12
* Add Beta support for Nintendo 3DS
* Introduce Direct Loading for PSP and 3DS
* Add support for external keyboards
* Add support for gamepad and device rumble
* Opening game menu now requires a long press
* Slight improvements to touch controls layout and behaviour
* Expose sync save button
* Update all cores and game database
* More fixes

Fix for silent crash when closing a game
Fix for some games unnecessarily copied into cache directory

* Added support for WonderSwan / Color
* Added support for low-latency audio
* Improved touch controls detection of multiple button presses
* Improved touch controls haptics
* Avoid copying PSX games into cache directory
* Updated all cores and databases
* Minor fixes and improvements

* Added support for Neo Geo Pocket /Color
* Added support for Sega CD (BIOSes required)
* Allow mapping tilt movements to DPAD, L1/R1 or L2/R2 pairs
* Fixed crashes when launching PSP games
* Allow disabling single gamepads
* Fixed issues with multi-track cue/bin files
* Improved disk scanning for PSX and PSP
* Updated all cores and databases
* Updated translations
* Fixed black screen on DS when gamepad connected
* Small fixes and improvements

* Add support for PC Engine
* Add support for Atari 7800
* Add support for Atari Lynx (BIOS required)
* Add IT and PT-BR translations
* Cores are now downloaded on demand from Google Play
* Use different overlays for different gamepads:
  - Playstation Standard vs Dualshock
  - Arcade now defaults to 4 buttons (6 buttons available)
  - Genesis now defaults to 3 buttons (6 buttons available)
* Added the possibility to support the application via IAP purchases (Google Play only)
* Updated cores and databases
* More...

* Sync/Back up saves and states via Google Drive (Google Play version only)
* Expose recent games on a TV channel
* Support pinning game shortcuts to app launcher
* Expose more options (such as PSP and N64 upscaling)
* Improved UI when covers are missing
* Improved app animations
* Allow pressing multiple front-facing buttons with one finger
* Fix issues with some gamepads detected as player 2
* Fix issues with missing saves on GBA and PSX
* Updated databases
* Updated cores
* Many smaller fixes and improvements

New Nintendo DS core (MelonDS)
    * 2x the performances of DeSmuME
    * Saves are not compatible with DeSmuME
    * Enable it from Settings -> Change Cores -> MelonDS

Add support for MAME2003 Plus core
    * Support MAME 0.78 romsets
    * Use full non-merged roms for auto-detection

Redesigned game menu
Improved touch controls customization experience
Improved Arcade and MD/Genesis layouts
Fix games not scanned on Google Chromecast with Android TV

* Fix issues with some PSP games running too fast
* Fix missing trigger events with some gamepads
* Fix missing gamepads events in some scenarios
* Fix touch controls not displayed because of ghosts gamepads
* Play audio while fast-forwarding
* Fix wrong aspect ratio with Genesis games
* Improved error handling with clearer messages
* Small fixes and improvements

* Add fast-forward option
* Add mute option
* Reimplemented audio from scratch to be more robust to glitches
* Fix potential crash when loading PSP games
* Fix screen flickering with some PSP games
* Fix crash while playing Drill Dozer on GBA
* Change n64 resolution to the original 320x240
* Exposed dynamic recompiler option for PSX (Enable it for better performances)
* Updated games database
* Small misc improvements and fixes

* Added support for chd and cue/bin format (PSX)
* Added support for multi-disk games with m3u playlists (PSX)
* Added support for favorites on Andorid TV
* Improved LCD filter which now looks sharper
* Allow changing display filter (Smooth, Sharp, LCD or CRT)
* Improved scanning performances (on my directory it's around 3 times faster)
* Cores are now bundled within the application
* Many small tweaks and improvements

Here you can find apks for all architectures. If you are unsure which one you should download pick the universal.

Touch controls completely redesigned from the ground up, with much better layout, proper multitouch support and bigger touch areas
Customizable in size and positioning (both landscape and portrait)
Clickable thumbsticks (double press and hold)
Sega Genesis/Mega Drive now uses the 6 buttons layout
Reduced area for diagonals detection in DPad
Tilt to stick is now enabled with a triple press on any stick
Some fixes and improvements

Allow customization of gamepad bindings
Automatically detect PSX BIOS in ROMs folder (this increases game compatibility)
Fix autosave for PSP
Reduce minimum OpenGLES version to 2.0 (2D cores can now run on older devices)
Hide empty sections on TV

releases.name: GameHub Brasil v2.0
releases.body:
# 🎮 GameHub Brasil v2.0 🇧🇷

## 🌟 Destaques desta Versão O GameHub Brasil v2.0 chega para revolucionar como você gerencia seus jogos e drivers. Focamos totalmente na flexibilidade e compatibilidade, permitindo que você importe seus próprios arquivos, gerencie perfis de controle com facilidade e leve a experiência de jogos para dispositivos de qualquer gama de desempenho. --- 🚀 Novidades e Recursos Implementados --- ## 📥AdrenoHub Drivers (Adrenotools). ✅ Integração Adrenotools: Sistema robusto para gerenciamento de drivers Vulkan. 📂 Importação Flexível: Suporte para importação de drivers Turnip no formato .zip. 💾 Fontes Variadas: Carregue drivers de fontes externas ou diretamente do armazenamento local do seu dispositivo. ## 📂 Contents Hub Centralize seus conteúdos de forma prática e rápida. 🎮 Formato .wcp: Importação completa de Contents no formato proprietário. 🔄 Compatibilidade Híbrida: Suporte a arquivos externos e locais. ⚠️ Nota Técnica: O importador de conteúdo está funcional, porém, a importação do **Wine** ainda enfrenta limitações devido a diferenças de arquitetura, estando em aperfeiçoamento. ## 🕹️ Personalização de Controle Jogue do seu jeito, sem precisar configurar tudo do zero. 📥 Importação de Perfis: Adicione perfis de controle personalizados para jogos específicos. 🌐 Fontes Externas: Compatibilidade com perfis baixados da comunidade. 🎯 Praticidade: Aplicação rápida para agilizar o setup dos seus jogos favoritos. ## 💾 Suporte a Armazenamento Externo (OTG) Mais espaço e liberdade para sua biblioteca de jogos. 🔌 Reconhecimento OTG: O app agora detecta e gerencia perfeitamente pen drives e HDs externos conectados via OTG. 📂 Jogar Externo: Instale e rode seus jogos diretamente do armazenamento removível. ## ⚡ LowSpecsExperienceV13 Leve jogos pesados para hardwares mais modestos. 📉 Otimização Gráfica: Programa dedicado a reduzir e otimizar jogos. 🎮 Foco em Jogabilidade: Prioriza FPS e estabilidade em detrimento de efeitos pesados. 📱 Inclusão Total: Já incluído nativamente nesta versão em Componentes. ## ✅ Wrappers e Vortek A ponte entre o sistema Android e seus jogos. 🛠️ Gerenciamento Avançado: Inclusão do sistema de Wrappers e Vortek, similar ao encontrado no Winlator. 🔁 Seleção de Drivers: Permite escolher a camada de compatibilidade ideal para cada título. ## 🔧 Melhorias Técnicas Esta versão focou na arquitetura interna do aplicativo para suportar as novas funções de importação: 🔨 Refatoração do Gerenciador de Arquivos: Melhorias na leitura de caminhos locais e externos. 🛡️ Estabilidade em Importações: Tratamento de erros aprimorado ao ler arquivos .zip e .wcp ## 📥 Informações de Download Nome do Arquivo: GameHub-Brasil-v2.0.apk Requisitos Mínimos: Android 8.0 (Oreo) ou superior 4GB de RAM (Recomendado 6GB+ para uso do LowSpecsExperience) 3GB de espaço livre (Para instalação e drivers) GPU com suporte a Vulkan 1.1+ Dispositivo com suporte a OTG (Opcional, para armazenamento externo) ## 🙏 Agradecimentos Essa versão é um grande passo em direção à personalização. Agradecemos a todos os testadores que validaram as novas funções e algumas que foram importadas do Winlator Ludashi [StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi) E para você ficar por dentro do houve no app, segue o vídeo do nosso parceiro [BrunoAndroid](https://www.youtube.com/@BrunoAndroid) explicando sobre o funcionamento do app:

## 📞 Suporte e Feedback Encontrou algum bug na importação de drivers ou no Contents Hub? 🐛 Reporte problemas na aba [Issues](../../issues) 💡 Compartilhe seus perfis de controle e wrappers na comunidade 💡 Compartilhe ideias em [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@winlatorbrasil) ⭐ Se gostou da atualização, deixe uma estrela no repositório! --- ## 📥 Sobre os pacotes que você deve baixar | Posição (prioridade) | Nome / Categoria | Package name | Motivo de uso (performance / detecção) | |---------------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Futuremark | com.futuremark.dmandroid.application | Benchmark GPU moderno (Wild Life / Solar Bay), referência para modo gamer em vários aparelhos atuais, excelente para GPUs Mali/Adreno. | | 2 | GFXBench | com.gfxbench.glbenchmark | Benchmark gráfico com cenários antigos e atuais (T-Rex, Manhattan, etc.), excelente para GPUs Mali/Adreno. | | 3 | AnTuTu | com.antutu.ABenchMark | Benchmark geral muito popular (CPU + GPU + RAM + UX), frequentemente usado para liberar modo turbo em ROMs chinesas. | | 4 | Ludashi | com.ludashi.benchmark | Benchmark chinês focado em score geral e otimização, conhecido por acionar perfis especiais em alguns devices e ROMs custom. | | 5 | Redmagic | com.riotgames.league.wildrift | Usado por fabricantes para ativar modos de alta performance e alta taxa de quadros. | | 6 | MiHoYo | com.mihoyo.yuanshen | Usado como “teste real” de SoC e gatilho de perfil gamer. | | 7 | PUBG | com.tencent.ig | Tradicionalmente incluído em listas de otimização para reduzir lag e melhorar estabilidade de FPS. | | 8 | GameHub Brasil | com.gamehub.brasil | App genérico/launcher para cenário “normal”, útil para manter um perfil padrão sem overclock ou boost ativado. | ---
**Desenvolvido com ❤️ para a comunidade gaming brasileira** *Jogue mais, jogue melhor!* **V2.0: Mais liberdade, mais jogos!**

releases.name: GameHub Brasil v1.0
releases.body:
# 🎮 GameHub Brasil v1.0 🇧🇷

--- ## 🌟 Destaques desta Versão Esta versão representa um marco significativo em otimização e estabilidade, trazendo melhorias substanciais de performance para todos os chipsets suportados, além de uma experiência visual completamente renovada e novos componentes de emulação de última geração. --- ## 📦 Novos Componentes e Drivers ### **Box64** - Camada de Tradução x86_64 Atualizações importantes para melhor compatibilidade e performance: - ✨ **Box64 0.3.8** - Versão estável com correções críticas - 🚀 **Box64 0.3.9-d730610** - Build de desenvolvimento com otimizações avançadas - ⚡ **WowBox64 0.3.9-d730610** - Variante otimizada para gaming ### **DXVK** - Camada de Tradução DirectX para Vulkan Nova série com GPLAsync e Link-Lazy para máxima performance: - 🎯 **DXVK-GPLAsync-LL 2.6.3-1** - Base estável com async pipeline - 🔧 **DXVK-GPLAsync-LL 2.6.4-1** - Correções e melhorias incrementais - 💎 **DXVK-GPLAsync-LL 2.7.1-1** - Versão mais recente com otimizações de shader ### **FEX-Emu** - Emulador x86/x86_64 - 🆕 **FEXCore-2510** - Última geração do núcleo de emulação com melhorias significativas de velocidade ### **Turnip** - Driver Vulkan Mesa para Adreno Suporte completo ao Mesa 26.0.0: - 🔥 **Turnip v26.0.0 R1 Autotuner** - Ajuste automático de performance baseado em GPU - ⚙️ **Turnip v26.0.0 R1** - Versão padrão otimizada --- ## ⚡ Otimizações de Performance por Chipset Esta versão inclui ajustes específicos para extrair o máximo desempenho de cada arquitetura: ### 🔥 **Snapdragon (Qualcomm)** - Otimizações para GPUs Adreno - Perfis customizados para séries 6xx, 7xx e 8xx - Melhor aproveitamento de cache L2/L3 ### 🚀 **Dimensity / Helio (MediaTek)** - Ajustes para arquitetura ARM Mali - Balanceamento de carga entre clusters CPU - Otimização de thermal throttling ### 💫 **Exynos (Samsung)** - Perfis específicos para Mali e Xclipse - Gerenciamento inteligente de frequências - Compatibilidade aprimorada com drivers --- ## 🎨 Renovação Visual Completa ### **Interface Modernizada** - 🖼️ **Novo Wallpaper Principal** - Design moderno e imersivo - 🎬 **Intro Redesenhada** - Animação de inicialização atualizada - 🎨 **Paleta de Cores Renovada** - Esquema cromático mais harmônico e profissional - 🌈 **Wallpapers Adicionais** - Nova coleção de papéis de parede temáticos ### **Seção "Sobre" Aprimorada** - 📝 HTML customizado em "Informações Pessoais" - 💼 Layout mais profissional e organizado - ℹ️ Informações atualizadas e detalhadas --- ## 🌐 Novos Recursos ### **Feed de Notícias & Jogos Grátis** - 📰 Fique atualizado com as últimas notícias do mundo dos games - 🎁 Descubra jogos gratuitos disponíveis - 🔔 Sistema de notificações para novos conteúdos ### **Tradução PT-BR Automática** - 🇧🇷 Interface completamente em Português do Brasil - 🔤 Tradução inteligente de conteúdos dinâmicos --- ## 🔧 Melhorias Técnicas e Correções ### **Otimização de Conectividade** - ❌ **Removida integração direta com GitHub** - Solução definitiva para erro "429: Too Many Requests" - Eliminação de problemas de carregamento do menu ### **Estabilidade Geral** - 🛡️ Correções de bugs críticos - ⚙️ Gerenciamento de recursos otimizado --- ## 📥 Informações de Download **Nome do Arquivo:** `GameHub-Brasil-v1.0.apk` **Requisitos Mínimos:** - Android 8.0 (Oreo) ou superior - 2GB de RAM (4GB recomendado) - 500MB de espaço livre - GPU com suporte a Vulkan 1.1+ --- ## 🙏 Agradecimentos Agradecemos a toda comunidade GameHub Brasil pelo feedback contínuo e suporte. Esta versão é resultado direto das sugestões e relatórios de vocês! E ao GameHub Lite 🤜🤛. --- ## 📞 Suporte e Feedback Encontrou algum problema ou tem sugestões? - 🐛 Reporte bugs na aba [Issues](../../issues) - 💡 Compartilhe ideias em [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@winlatorbrasil) - ⭐ Se gostou, deixe uma estrela no repositório! ---
**Desenvolvido com ❤️ para a comunidade gaming brasileira** *Jogue mais, jogue melhor!*

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.8.0
releases.body:
> ⚠️ **Only install stable releases. Do NOT install pre-releases or CI artifacts.** Pre-releases may be incomplete, unstable, or contain untested changes. Stable releases have been fully tested on real hardware.

## New since v2.7.6 (last stable)

- **Epic Games Store tab** — full integration: OAuth2 login, library sync with title/developer/art/description, 6-parallel chunk downloads, SHA-1 integrity verify, file assembly, launch via EditImportedGameInfoDialog
- **Japanese translations** — complete 3,468-string Japanese locale (Crowdin source, contributed by reindex-ot); app displays in Japanese when system language is set to Japanese
- **Download speed + filename** — Epic, Amazon, and GOG all show the current filename and MB/s speed during download
- **Epic install size fix** — correctly shows uncompressed install size instead of compressed CDN chunk size
- **Uninstall checkmark fix** — both the collapsed header ✓ and expanded section ✓ disappear immediately on uninstall in Epic, Amazon, and GOG
- **Amazon download progress fix** — filename now displays correctly during download
- **Amazon OTP / 2FA login fix** — login no longer hangs after entering a one-time password or security code

## Full Feature Set

- **Steam** — My Games tab, library, launch (full GameHub native)
- **GOG Games tab** — OAuth2 login, library sync with cover art, Gen2/Gen1/installer download, install progress with speed + filename, launch, uninstall, Copy to Downloads
- **Amazon Games tab** — PKCE OAuth2 login (OTP/2FA supported), library sync, manifest.proto download (XZ/LZMA, 6-parallel), SHA-256 verify, launch via fuel.json, SDK DLLs, update checker
- **Epic Games Store tab** — OAuth2 login, library sync, chunked manifest download (6-parallel), SHA-1 verify, file assembly, launch
- **Download speed + filename** — all three stores show current filename and MB/s during download
- **Components Manager** — backup, inject WCP/ZIP, Remove, Add New Component
- **Download from Online Repos** — 3-level navigator: repo → category → asset → download + inject
- **Performance sidebar** — Sustained Performance mode, Max Adreno Clocks toggles
- **RTS touch controls** — credit @Nightwalker743
- **Winlator HUD overlay** — FPS/frame time/resolution, opacity slider, position/orientation persistence
- **Japanese locale** — complete 3,468-string translation (Crowdin, contributed by reindex-ot)
- **"My Games" tab label** fix
- **BCI launcher button** — top-right toolbar shortcut to BannersComponentInjector

## Credits

- **GOG, Amazon Games, and Epic Games Store integrations** — [The GameNative Team](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative). The API pipelines, authentication flows, download architectures, and library sync for all three stores in BannerHub are based on their research and implementation.
- **Japanese translations** — [reindex-ot](https://github.com/reindex-ot) (via Crowdin)
- **RTS Touch Controls** — [@Nightwalker743](https://github.com/Nightwalker743)
- **GameHub ReVanced patches** — [@playday3008](https://github.com/playday3008/gamehub-patches)

## Installation

Install the APK matching your use case:
- **Normal (`banner.hub`)** — standard install, coexists with GameHub
- **Normal(GHL) (`gamehub.lite`)** — coexists with BannerHub Lite
- Other variants (PuBG, Genshin, AnTuTu, etc.) — replace those specific apps

All APKs signed with AOSP testkey.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.7.6
releases.body: > ⚠️ **Only install stable releases. Do NOT install pre-releases or raw workflow artifacts.** Pre-releases are unfinished testing builds — they may be unstable, crash, or contain broken features. Always use the latest stable release for normal use. --- ## New since v2.7.5 (last stable) ### Amazon Games Integration - **Amazon Games** side menu entry — full library with list/grid/poster views - **PKCE OAuth login** via Amazon's authorization server (no password stored) - **Library sync** — fetches all owned games via GetEntitlements API (paginated) - **Install confirm dialog** — shows game size (fetched from manifest) + available storage - **Download pipeline** — manifest.proto (XZ/LZMA), 6-parallel chunks, SHA-256 verification, resume support - **Progress bar** with percentage + current filename during download, Cancel button - **Exe picker** — shown post-install when multiple executables found; scoring heuristic auto-selects best candidate for single-exe games - **Launch** — adds game to My Games via LandscapeLauncherMainActivity - **Update checker** — compares installed versionId against live Amazon API; shows orange "Update Available" badge - **SDK DLLs** — auto-downloads FuelSDK_x64.dll + AmazonGamesSDK DLLs into Wine prefix ProgramData before launch - **Token refresh** — lazy re-auth when token is within 5 min of expiry - **Uninstall + Set .exe** from detail dialog - **Credits** — Amazon integration based on research by [The GameNative Team](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative) --- ## Full Feature Set ### Game Libraries - **Steam** — My Games tab with Steam library sync and launch - **GOG Games** — full library, OAuth2 login, download, install, launch, uninstall *(credit: The GameNative Team)* - **Amazon Games** — full library, PKCE login, download, install, launch, uninstall *(credit: The GameNative Team)* ### Component Manager - Install, remove, and inject Wine components (DXVK, VKD3D, Box64, GPU drivers) - Download from online repos (GitHub Releases API + pack.json sources) - Backup and restore components - Add/inject custom WCP and ZIP files ### Performance & Settings - Sustained Performance mode toggle - Max Adreno Clocks toggle - Winlator HUD overlay with opacity slider, text outline tiers, position and orientation persistence - Grant Root Access button in settings ### UI - "My Games" tab label (instead of "My") - BCI launcher button in top-right toolbar - RTS touch controls *(credit: @Nightwalker743)* - Per-variant app labels (BannerHub, BannerHub PuBG, BannerHub AnTuTu, etc.) - Offline Steam login skip --- ## Installation **Choose your APK by architecture:** - `BannerHub-v2.7.6-Normal.apk` — replaces standard GameHub (`banner.hub`) - `BannerHub-v2.7.6-Normal(GHL).apk` — coexists alongside GameHub Lite (`gamehub.lite`) - Other variants (PuBG, AnTuTu, Genshin, etc.) replace their respective apps **To coexist with the original GameHub:** install the `Normal(GHL)` variant — it uses package ID `gamehub.lite` and can be installed alongside the original app.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.7.5
releases.body:
> ⚠️ **IMPORTANT: Only install stable releases. Do NOT install pre-releases or CI artifacts.**
> Pre-releases are untested intermediate builds. Only stable releases (like this one) are verified working APKs safe to install on your device.

---

## New since v2.7.3 (last stable)

### Winlator-style HUD Overlay
- **Live HUD overlay** injected into the game window showing: API | TIME | GPU% | CPU% | RAM% | BAT | TMP | FPS + graph
- **Tap to toggle** between compact horizontal bar and vertical column layout
- **Drag to reposition** anywhere on screen — position persists across sessions
- **Orientation persists** — HUD remembers horizontal/vertical mode across sidebar opens
- **Time display** shown after API value in both horizontal and vertical modes

### Performance Sidebar (Settings → Performance)
- **HUD Opacity slider** (0–100%, default 80%) — controls background transparency only; text always stays fully opaque
- **"Extra Detailed (coming soon)"** checkbox — grayed out, reserved for a future per-core + GPU detail view
- **Opacity auto-restores** on every game launch

### HUD Readability at Low Opacity
- **≥ 30%** — clean text, no effect
- **10–29%** — black shadow halo (blurred glow) around text
- **< 10%** — solid black stroke outline + shadow halo combined for maximum readability

---

## Full Feature Set

- **"My Games" tab** relabeled to "My Games"
- **BCI launcher button** (top-right toolbar) — quick launch to BannersComponentInjector
- **Components side menu** — backup, inject WCP/ZIP, add new component, remove component
- **In-app component downloader** — browse GitHub Releases repos + WCPHub by category (DXVK, VKD3D, Box64, FEXCore, GPU Driver); download and auto-inject
- **GOG integration** — OAuth2 login, library sync, game cards with ✓ Installed checkmark, Gen 1 + Gen 2 download, exe picker, uninstall
- **Epic Games integration** — OAuth2 login, library sync, chunked manifest download, exe picker, install confirmation, cancel download, Set .exe button
- **RTS touch controls** — gesture-based touch overlay for RTS games (credit @Nightwalker743)
- **Sustained Performance toggle** — requires root
- **Max Adreno Clocks toggle** — requires root
- **Winlator HUD overlay** — live performance stats, tap to toggle H/V, drag to reposition, opacity slider, text outline at low opacity
- **API Source selector** — switch between BannerHub API and EmuReady Proxy
- **Offline Steam login skip** — bypasses Steam login when offline
- **Per-variant package names** — Normal (`banner.hub`), Normal GHL (`gamehub.lite`), + 7 game-specific variants

---

## Installation

Install the APK matching your use case:
- **BannerHub-v2.7.5-Normal.apk** — standard install, package `banner.hub` (coexists with GameHub)
- **BannerHub-v2.7.5-Normal(GHL).apk** — package `gamehub.lite` (coexists with both GameHub and BannerHub Normal)
- Other variants (PuBG, Genshin, AnTuTu, etc.) use game-specific package names for simultaneous installs

All APKs are signed with AOSP testkey (v1/v2/v3). Install via ADB or any file manager that supports unknown sources.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.7.4
releases.body: > [!WARNING] > **Only install stable releases (like this one).** Do NOT install pre-releases or raw CI artifacts — they are incomplete test builds that may crash, corrupt data, or break your Wine containers. Stable releases are the only builds that have been tested end-to-end. --- ## New since v2.7.3 (last stable) ### Wine Task Manager — Container Info + dual-tab process viewer The sidebar Task Manager has been completely rebuilt. A persistent **Container Info** panel sits at the top at all times, showing live container-specific metrics: | Row | What it shows | |-----|---------------| | **CPU Cores** | Cores assigned to the container / total device cores — reads the `WINEMU_CPU_AFFINITY` affinity bitmask from the wine child process and counts set bits (e.g. "2 / 8 total") | | **Sys RAM** | Device system RAM: used MB / total MB (from `/proc/meminfo`) | | **VRam Limit** | The VRam budget set in Game Settings → Compatibility, read directly from SharedPreferences (e.g. "1024 MB" or "Unlimited") | | **Device** | Device model name | | **Android** | Android version + API level | Below the Container Info, two tabs let you browse running processes: - **Applications** — Wine infrastructure processes (wineserver, explorer, etc.) - **Processes** — Windows `.exe` processes running under Wine - Each row shows the process name, PID, and a **Kill** button - A **↺** button manually refreshes the list; the panel also auto-refreshes every 3 seconds while open > **Credit:** Performance overlay concept and inspiration from [@StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ) (Winlator performance overlay). --- ### API Source selector — radio-button dialog The EmuReady toggle in Settings has been replaced with a proper **3-option radio-button dialog**. Tap the toggle to open it and choose which backend API BannerHub uses: | Option | What it does | |--------|-------------| | **BannerHub API** | Uses the self-hosted BannerHub static API (default — always works, no login required) | | **GameHub Live API** | Passes requests through to the real GameHub server (requires a valid GameHub session) | | **EmuReady API** | Uses the EmuReady community compatibility database | Your selection is saved immediately, caches are cleared, and a confirmation toast is shown. You can change it at any time without restarting. --- ## BannerHub API — Component Catalog The in-app **Component Downloader** (Component Manager → Download) pulls from the [BannerHub API](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-api). All components below are available directly inside the app — no browser or manual file transfer needed. ### DXVK (42 builds) | Build | Notes | |-------|-------| | DXVK-2.7.1-1-gplasync-arm64ec | Standard + gplasync | | DXVK-2.5.3-1-gplasync | Standard + gplasync | | DXVK-2.4.1-1-gplasync | Standard + gplasync | | DXVK-2.3.1-1-gplasync | Standard + gplasync | | dxvk-v2.7.1-1-async | async | | dxvk-2.7.1 | Standard | | dxvk-v2.6.2-1-async | async | | dxvk-v2.6-1-async | async | | dxvk-v2.5.2-1-async | async | | dxvk-v2.4.1-async | async | | dxvk-v2.3.1-async | async | | dxvk-2.6-arm64ec-async | arm64ec + async | | dxvk-2.4.1-arm64ec | arm64ec | | dxvk-2.3.1-arm64ec-async | arm64ec + async | | dxvk-2.3.1-arm64ec | arm64ec | | dxvk-1.10.3-arm64ec-async | arm64ec + ### Box64 / FEXCore (31 builds) | Build | Notes | |-------|-------| | FEXCore-2603 | Latest FEX | | FEXCore-2601-aabdded | FEX | | FEXCore-2510.1 | FEX | | FEXCore-2509 | FEX | | FEXCore-2508 | FEX | into three groups in the API: **Mesa Turnip (Gen8 / A8XX / MTR / misc)** — open-source Mesa Vulkan driver for Qualcomm Adreno: - Mesa Turnip Gen8 V10 through V26 (all revisions), including autotuner and MR variants - A8XX MR v12 through v23, gralloc-exp, vdont_use - A8XX Draft v11, v11-sdkfix - Mesa-git Turnip v26.1.0-git - Mesa 26.0 Turnip v1, v2 - Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git hotfixes - Turnip-v26.1.0 R1–R5, b1–b8 - Turnip-v26.0.0 R1–R8 (including _mem variants) - Turnip-v25.x.x R1–R13 (v25.0–v25.3, including _mem and one_ui7 variants) - Turnip-v24.x.x R2–R12 - Turnip a6xx, OneUI, Normal (26.0.0-devel) - Turnip MTR v1.5 through v3.2.0 (A8XX / A840P / A840 variants, both performance and balanced) - Various single builds: PixelyIon, Deck, Dec 31 2025, AutoTuner, MR38808, Gen8-Hacks, etc. **Qualcomm official (closed-source):** - qcom-849.0 - qcom-842.1, 842.13, 842.16, 842.19 - qcom-840 - qcom-837.6 - qcom-863.1 (Snapdragon 8 Elite) - qcom-800.26, 800.30, 800.33–800.35, 800.36, 800.40, 800.46, 800.51, 800.64 - 8eGen5-842.6, 842.8 - 8Elite-800.21, 800.22, 800.26, 800.30, 800.33–800.36, 800.40, 800.46, 800.51 - Adreno_762.10, Adreno_805.0, Adreno_814, Adreno_819 - Qualcomm 863.1, v849 (two variants) ### Libraries (100+ entries) Winetricks-style runtime libraries installable into your Wine prefix — selected highlights: `vcredist2005` `vcredist2008` `vcredist2010` `vcredist2012` `vcredist2013` `vcredist2015` `vcredist2019` `vcredist2022` `dotnet20` `dotnet35` `dotnet35sp1` `dotnet40` `dotnet45` `dotnet46` `dotnet452` `dotnet461` `dotnet462` `dotnet472` `dotnet48` `dotnet50` `dotnetcore3` `dotnetcoredesktop3` `dotnetcoredesktop6` `dotnetcoredesktop7` `dotnetcoredesktop8` `mono` `mono-10.1.0` `mono-10.3.0` `mono-10.4.1` `gecko` `xna31` `xna40` `xinput` `d3dcompiler_42` `d3dcompiler_43` `d3dcompiler_46` `d3dcompiler_47` `d3dx9` `d3dx11` `physx` `mediafoundation` `directplay` `directmusic` `directshow` `dsound` `dswave` `xact` `xact_x64` `oalinst` `VulkanRT` `webview2` `powershell` `powershell_core` `sqlite3` `msxml3` `msxml4` `msxml6` `K-Lite` `lavfilters702` `lavfilters741` `ffdshow` `dirac` `quicktime72` `cnc-ddraw` `cjkfonts` `winXP` `win7` `gdiplus` `vcrun6` `vbrun6` `mfc40` `mfc42` `mdac28` `jet40` `XLiveRedist` `gfw` — and more. > Full component catalog and source files: [github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-api](https://github.com/The412Banner/bannerhub-api) --- ## Full Feature Set - **My Games tab** — renamed from "My" to "My Games" for clarity - **BCI Launcher button** — top-right toolbar shortcut that opens BannersComponentInjector directly from within a game session - **Component Manager** (side menu → Components): - View all installed WCP/ZIP components in a scrollable list - Backup a component to Downloads - Inject a WCP or ZIP file from local storage - **Add New Component** — true injection via ComponentInjectorHelper - Remove an installed component - **Component Downloader** — "↓ Download from Online Repos" in the Component Manager type-selection menu: - 3-level navigation: choose a repo → choose a category (DXVK / VKD3D / Box64 / FEXCore / GPU Driver) → browse assets → download and auto-inject - Sources include Nightlies by The412Banner, Arihany WCPHub, and more - **RTS Touch Controls** — real-time strategy optimised touch input (credit [@Nightwalker743](https://github.com/Nightwalker743)) - **Offline Steam login skip** — bypasses the Steam login screen when offline so you can still launch your library - **GOG Integration** (side menu → GOG): - OAuth2 login via WebView (token stored securely in SharedPreferences) - Library sync — fetches all owned GOG games and displays them as cards - Gen 1 and Gen 2 download pipeline with progress indicator - Install confirmation dialog (shows download size + available storage before starting) - Cancel download — stops the thread and cleans up partial files - Post-install exe picker — auto-selects if one candidate; dialog if multiple - Set .exe button in game detail dialog — re-scan install dir and update at any time - Launch installed games directly from the GOG tab - Uninstall support with confirmation - ✓ Installed checkmark on game cards - **Wine Task Manager** — sidebar panel (see above) - **API Source selector** — 3-option dialog (see above) --- ## Installation > **AnTuTu note:** Two AnTuTu APK variants are provided: > - `AnTuTu` — package `com.antutu.ABenchMark` (original AnTuTu package name) > - `alt-AnTuTu` — package `com.antutu.benchmark.full` — use this if you are using the **Beacon** front end, as the original AnTuTu package name causes compatibility issues with Beacon Download the APK that matches your device architecture: | File | CPU | Notes | |------|-----|-------| | `BannerHub-Normal-arm64-v8a.apk` | ARM64 | **Most devices — use this** | | `BannerHub-Normal-armeabi-v7a.apk` | ARMv7 32-bit | Older devices | | `BannerHub-Normal-x86_64.apk` | x86_64 | Intel/AMD tablets | | `BannerHub-Normal(GHL)-*.apk` | Any | Installs as `gamehub.lite` package — coexists with the standard build | > The `Normal(GHL)` variant uses the `gamehub.lite` package name so it can be installed alongside the regular `banner.hub` build without conflict.

releases.name: Bannerhub v2.7.3
releases.body: > ⚠️ **Only install STABLE releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or workflow artifacts — pre-releases are untested intermediate builds used for debugging, and unsigned artifacts are not properly signed for device installation. --- ## New since v2.7.2 (last stable) - **Install confirmation dialog** — before any GOG game download starts, shows the game's download size and your available storage so you can confirm before committing - **Cancel download button** — Install button turns red and becomes Cancel during an active download; pressing it stops the thread and deletes all partial files from the install directory - **Exe picker dialog (post-install)** — after install completes, if 2 or more qualifying executables are found, a dialog lets you pick the correct one (excludes redist/setup/unins/crash/helper/dotnet/vcredist/directx paths) - **Set .exe button in game detail dialog** — long-press any installed game → detail dialog now shows the current .exe filename and a **Set .exe…** button to re-pick from the install directory at any time --- ## Full Feature Set ### GOG Games (Side Menu) - OAuth2 login via WebView — token stored, auto-refreshed, survives restarts - Full library sync — Gen 1 (pre-Galaxy) and Gen 2 (Galaxy) games - **3 view modes**: List / Grid / Poster toggle - **SteamGridDB cover art** (600×900) with GOG icon fallback - **Long-press** any tile → detail dialog (description, developer, genre, current .exe, Set .exe… button) - Gen 2 download: depot manifest → per-file CDN download with live progress - Gen 1 download: byte-range resumable download - Installer fallback for very old pre-Galaxy titles (no content-system builds) - **Confirmation dialog** before download (size + available storage) - Live download progress: filename + percentage - **Cancel download** button (red during download — stops thread + deletes partial files) - **Exe picker** after install (auto-selects if only 1 candidate; dialog if 2+) - **Set .exe** button in detail dialog — re-pick the launch executable at any time - **Add** button after install → opens `EditImportedGameInfoDialog` pre-filled with exe path - Installed checkmark on cards (persistent across restarts) - Copy to Downloads folder button in detail dialog - Uninstall button in detail dialog ### Component Manager (Side Menu) - Color-coded component cards (DXVK/VKD3D/Box64/FEXCore/GPU Driver/WCP) - Live search bar - Inject WCP or ZIP to replace an existing component - Add New Component (true injection — appears in GameHub menus immediately) - Swipe right → Backup to Downloads - Swipe left → Remove (unregisters + deletes) - Remove All BannerHub-managed components (stock components untouched) - Source badge on downloaded components ### In-App Component Downloader - 3-level nav: Repo → Category → Asset list - Built-in sources: Arihany WCPHub, The412Banner Nightlies, Kimchi, StevenMXZ, and more - Checkmark on already-installed assets - One-tap download + inject with live progress ### Performance Sidebar - Sustained Performance Mode toggle - Max Adreno GPU Clocks toggle ### Other Features - **BCI Launcher button** — top-right toolbar shortcut to BannersComponentInjector - **"My Games" tab label** — renamed from "My" for clarity - **Offline Steam launch** — skips Steam login screen when offline - **Grant Root Access** button in Settings → Advanced - **RTS Touch Controls** — customizable touch input for RTS games (credit @Nightwalker743) - **Component descriptions** in Game Settings (blurb shown for each component) --- ## Installation > **AnTuTu note:** Two AnTuTu APK variants are provided: > - `AnTuTu` — package `com.antutu.ABenchMark` (original AnTuTu package name) > - `alt-AnTuTu` — package `com.antutu.benchmark.full` — use this if you are using the **Beacon** front end, as the original AnTuTu package name causes compatibility issues with Beacon | APK | Package | Use when | |-----|---------|----------| | `Normal.apk` | `banner.hub` | Fresh install / coexist with official GameHub Lite | | `Normal(GHL).apk` | `gamehub.lite` | Replace official GameHub Lite slot | | `PuBG.apk` | `com.tencent.ig` | Replace PuBG GameHub variant | | `AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.ABenchMark` | Replace AnTuTu variant | | `alt-AnTuTu.apk` | `com.antutu.benchmark.full` | Replace alt AnTuTu variant | | `PuBG-CrossFire.apk` | `com.tencent.tmgp.cf` | Replace CrossFire variant | | `Ludashi.apk` | `com.ludashi.aibench` | Replace Ludashi variant | | `Genshin.apk` | `com.mihoyo.genshinimpact` | Replace Genshin variant | | `Original.apk` | `com.xiaoji.egggame` | Replace Original variant | **If you have no GameHub variant installed:** use **Normal.apk** — it coexists with the official GameHub Lite app. All 9 variants can be installed simultaneously. All APKs are signed with AOSP testkey (v1/v2/v3).
